title: hieracium atratum subsp. pannucium
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. pannucium, also known as the spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and dark green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The spotted hawkweed is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
